tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and high urine protein and ketones, which may indicate renal dysfunction or metabolic stress. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and contains glucose and bilirubin, raising concern for possible urinary tract or hepatic involvement. HbA1c is low, possibly reflecting recent hypoglycemia or anemia. Recommend clinical correlation for renal, hepatic, and metabolic status, and further evaluation for urinary tract pathology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
